title: Kiesera minor
intro: Kiesera minor, also known as Kiefferi or Kidney Vetch, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia, belonging to the legume family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Kiesera minor include isoflavones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Kiesera minor is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, sore throats, and menstrual cramps.
